name: Vulnerability Count by Severity (Qualys)
natural_language_question: How many critical and high severity vulnerabilities do we have, and how has that changed over the last 30 days?
calculation_plan_steps: Start from the silver_qualys_vulnerabilities_daily table which contains one canonical row per vulnerability per asset per day
Filter to records where severity is CRITICAL or HIGH and state is OPEN
Group by severity and snapshot_date to compute daily counts for each severity tier
Restrict the output to the last 30 days and include rolling 7-day average to smooth short-term variance
Produce series lines for CRITICAL and HIGH to visualize trend over time
implementation_note: Requires silver_qualys_vulnerabilities_daily to be built from qualys_vulnerabilities ingest

name: Total Open Vulnerabilities (Qualys)
natural_language_question: What is the total number of open vulnerabilities across the environment and how is that trending over time?
calculation_plan_steps: Use silver_qualys_vulnerabilities_daily as the source of daily snapshots
Filter to records where state is OPEN/ACTIVE
Aggregate counts across all assets per snapshot_date to produce a daily total open vulnerabilities time series
Compute a 30-day moving average and prior-period comparison for trend context
implementation_note: Requires daily silver snapshots to compute moving averages and period comparisons

name: Vulnerabilities by Asset (Qualys)
natural_language_question: Which assets have the highest counts of vulnerabilities and how have those counts changed over the last quarter?
calculation_plan_steps: Start from silver_qualys_vulnerabilities_daily which contains per-asset vulnerability snapshots
Group by asset_id and snapshot_date to compute daily vulnerability counts per asset
Filter the date range to the last 90 days and compute change versus prior 90-day period for each asset
Rank assets by current vulnerability count and include asset metadata (owner, environment) for triage
implementation_note: Requires enrichment of assets with owner and environment in the silver table

name: Open Critical Vulnerabilities (30-day) (Qualys)
natural_language_question: How many critical vulnerabilities have been open in the environment over the last 30 days, and is the count increasing or decreasing?
calculation_plan_steps: Use silver_qualys_vulnerabilities_daily and filter to severity = CRITICAL and state = OPEN
For each snapshot_date, count unique vulnerability instances affecting assets
Limit the series to the last 30 days and compute daily deltas and a 7-day rolling average
Flag long-lived critical vulnerabilities (age > 14 days) separately for prioritization
implementation_note: Requires first_seen_date and fixed_date fields in silver table to compute age and status

name: Average Time to Remediate Vulnerabilities (Qualys)
natural_language_question: What is the average time to remediate vulnerabilities (MTTR) by severity over the last 90 days?
calculation_plan_steps: From silver_qualys_vulnerabilities_daily, identify vulnerability instances with both first_seen_date and fixed_date populated
Compute remediation_time_days for each closed vulnerability as fixed_date minus first_seen_date
Aggregate remediation_time_days by snapshot_date and severity to produce daily median and mean MTTR time series
Present series for each severity tier and compute a 90-day trend and prior-period comparison
implementation_note: Requires fixed_date and first_seen_date to be consistently populated in the silver table

name: Vulnerability Count by Severity (Snyk)
natural_language_question: How many high and critical dependency vulnerabilities are present in projects tracked by Snyk, and how is that changing over time?
calculation_plan_steps: Start from silver_snyk_vulnerabilities_daily which contains normalized Snyk vulnerability snapshots per project/component per day
Filter to severity levels HIGH and CRITICAL and where state indicates the vulnerability is unresolved
Group by severity and snapshot_date to produce a daily trend of counts per severity
Include project and language as dimensions for grouping and downstream filtering
implementation_note: Requires silver_snyk_vulnerabilities_daily to be produced by normalizing Snyk ingest to daily snapshots

name: Vulnerable Dependency Exposure (Snyk)
natural_language_question: What proportion of production projects have high or critical vulnerable dependencies and how is that proportion changing over the last 30 days?
calculation_plan_steps: Use silver_snyk_vulnerabilities_daily to identify vulnerable dependencies per project and tag exposure as direct or transitive
For each snapshot_date, compute the number of production projects with at least one HIGH/CRITICAL vulnerable dependency and the total production project count
Calculate daily proportion = projects_with_vulnerable_dependencies / total_production_projects
Produce a 30-day trend line and rolling average to show direction of exposure
kpis_covered: Proportion of projects with high/critical dependencies
implementation_note: Requires project environment metadata (production vs non-production) to be available in silver table

name: Patch Compliance Rate (Qualys)
natural_language_question: What percentage of patchable vulnerabilities are patched within the SLA window and how has that percentage trended over the last 90 days?
calculation_plan_steps: From silver_qualys_vulnerabilities_daily, filter to vulnerabilities marked as PATCHABLE
For each snapshot_date compute patched_count (PATCHABLE vulnerabilities with fixed_date populated within SLA window) and applicable_count (total PATCHABLE vulnerabilities)
Calculate daily patch_compliance_rate = patched_count / applicable_count
Produce a 90-day trend and highlight days where rate falls below expected thresholds
implementation_note: Partial measurement; full patch compliance may require integration with patch management systems for authoritative patch status

name: Assets Without Recent Scan
natural_language_question: How many assets have not been scanned in the last 90 days and how has that count changed over time?
calculation_plan_steps: From silver_qualys_vulnerabilities_daily derive last_scan_date per asset using the latest snapshot entry
Flag assets where last_scan_date is older than 90 days as stale_scan
Aggregate daily counts of stale_scan assets to produce a time series of unscanned assets
Include asset owner and environment in the output for prioritization and remediation planning
implementation_note: Requires reliable asset identifiers and scan timestamp fields in qualys ingest

name: CVE Exposure Age Distribution (Combined)
natural_language_question: How is CVE exposure distributed by age buckets (0-30, 31-90, 91-365, >365 days) across infrastructure and code dependencies, and how is this distribution trending?
calculation_plan_steps: Build silver_cve_exposure_daily by combining normalized CVE records from silver_qualys_vulnerabilities_daily and silver_snyk_vulnerabilities_daily
For each CVE instance compute age_in_days since first_seen_date and bucket into defined age ranges
Aggregate counts by bucket, severity, and source_system for each snapshot_date to form a time series matrix
Surface the matrix as a heatmap showing trends across age buckets and sources to highlight long-lived exposures
implementation_note: Requires both silver_qualys_vulnerabilities_daily and silver_snyk_vulnerabilities_daily to be normalized and joined by CVE
